title: Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva Temple
tagline: Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

heroImage.alt: Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva Temple, Kerala

overview.short: Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva Temple , an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva is situated on the banks of the Neyyar (river) at Amaravila of Neyyattinkara (tehsil) in Thiru
overview.description: Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva Temple , an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva is situated on the banks of the Neyyar (river) at Amaravila of Neyyattinkara (tehsil) in Thiruvananthapuram District in Kerala state in India. The presiding deity of the temple is Lord Rameswara facing west. It is believed that Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva temple is one of the 108 Shiva temples of Kerala and is installed by sage Parasurama dedicated to Shiva. The temple is located 1 km away from Neyyattinkara town in Amaravila village.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Neyyattinkara
topPlaces.description: The Diocese of Neyyattinkara (Latin: Neyyattinkaraen(sis)) is a diocese located in the town of Neyyattinkara in the ecclesiastical province of Trivandrum in India.

topPlaces.name: Sree Maha Ganapathy Temple
topPlaces.description: The Sree Maha Ganapathy temple is located in Thamarakulam, also known as the heart of Kollam. The main deity is Lord Vigneshwara, who is believed to be the person who takes responsibility of the whole region.

topPlaces.name: Panachamoodu
topPlaces.description: Panachamoodu is a village in Neyyattinkara Taluk in Trivandrum district, Kerala State, India. and also sharing its border with Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Amaravila
topPlaces.description: Amaravila is a village in Neyyattinkara town in Trivandrum district, Kerala State, India. Kerala's second largest checkpost after Walayar is situated in Amaravila.

topPlaces.name: Neyyattinkara
topPlaces.description: Neyyattinkara is a municipal town situated 18 km south east of Thiruvananthapuram city in Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, India. It also serves as the administrative headquarters of Neyyattinkara taluk.

topPlaces.name: Neyyattinkara taluk
topPlaces.description: Neyyattinkara is a Taluk (tehsil) located in the southern region of Thiruvananthapuram district, in the Indian state of Kerala. This taluk encompasses a total of 21 villages and one municipality, making it the southernmost taluk in the state.

topPlaces.name: Balaramapuram
topPlaces.description: Balaramapuram is one of the panchayats that form the city of Thiruvananthapuram, the capital of Kerala, India. It is the most urbanized panchayat in Thiruvananthapuram.

topPlaces.name: Anavoor
topPlaces.description: Anavoor is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (21 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (18 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Amaravila Rameswaram Sri Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Diocese of Neyyattinkara, Sree Maha Ganapathy Temple, Panachamoodu, Amaravila, Neyyattinkara.
